mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the 4-byte 'pop3' scheme literal from the CMP buffer and substitutes it directly into the input, routing the harness into the mime/userheader code path in one mutation.
one_line: I2S substitutes 'pop3' scheme literal to reach mime userheader loop

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace splices the 4-byte 'GDEF' OpenType table tag from the CMP buffer into the input at the right offset in one step, bypassing the all-or-nothing FOURCC equality that Hamming gradient cannot progressively climb.
one_line: I2S substitutes 'GDEF' FOURCC tag to enter switch case arm

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the 4-byte 'eXIf' chunk-type literal from the CMP buffer into the input chunk header, routing the dispatcher into png_handle_eXIf where the length<2 gate is reachable.
one_line: I2S plants 'eXIf' chunk tag so length<2 branch becomes reachable

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace captures the runtime 'xmlns' string operand from the CMP buffer and substitutes it into the input at the attribute local-name position, producing the exact unprefixed form that the xmlStrEqual gate requires.
one_line: I2S substitutes 'xmlns' literal into attribute local-name position

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace materializes the multi-byte IPv6 protocol-header template (32 bytes of concentrated literals) from CMP buffer operands in one mutation, pushing aOffset past kBufferDataSize to reach the false branch of the range check.
one_line: I2S materializes fixed IPv6 header template to exceed buffer size threshold

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts curl's scheme strcmp against 'pop3' (and HTTP/URL tokens) and substitutes those literals into the input, enabling the upload/connect path where the writefd revents false branch is reachable.
one_line: I2S substitutes 'pop3' scheme bytes to unlock upload/socket code path

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace captures the 4-byte 'GPOS' table tag from the CMP buffer and substitutes it into the input as a unit, setting apply_gpos=true so the else-if(apply_fallback_kern) branch takes its false side.
one_line: I2S inserts 'GPOS' tag so apply_gpos routes away from fallback-kern

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the 32-bit CMP against the png_hIST FOURCC constant and substitutes that literal into a chunk-name slot of an otherwise valid PNG, making the dispatch equality at line 189 fire true.
one_line: I2S substitutes 'hIST' FOURCC into PNG chunk name field

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the byte-by-byte CMPs against the UTF-8 BOM (0xEF, 0xBB, 0xBF) and substitutes all three constants into the first three input bytes in one mutation, satisfying the chained equality at line 987.
one_line: I2S substitutes UTF-8 BOM bytes to satisfy chained three-byte equality

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace observes the switch byte-CMP against the constant 'q' and substitutes it into the input at the entity-name first-byte position, routing the switch to the 'q' arm that naive cannot reach from havoc alone.
one_line: I2S substitutes 'q' byte into entity name to hit switch case arm

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the 16-byte Thread multicast address constant from the CMP buffer and pastes it into the IPv6 destination field of the input, satisfying the address equality that naive cannot synthesize from blind havoc.
one_line: I2S pastes 16-byte Thread multicast address into IPv6 destination field

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes 'HTTP/' and CRLF tokens from CMP buffer operands into the input, causing Curl_headers_push to populate the headers linked-list so the for-loop initializer sees a non-NULL head pointer.
one_line: I2S plants 'HTTP/' and CRLF to populate curl headers linked list

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts multiple sequential CMPs (scheme 'pop3+-', host '127.0.0.1', 'GHTTP/') and substitutes each constant into fixed offsets, enabling a real HTTP transfer so the byte-count sum becomes positive at line 1708.
one_line: I2S substitutes scheme/host/HTTP literals to produce nonzero byte counters

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace captures the 'HTTP/' literal from CMP operands logged during protocol-prefix parsing and substitutes it into the input, enabling sscanf to parse the status line and increment k->headerline so the false branch fires on subsequent iterations.
one_line: I2S plants 'HTTP/' so sscanf increments headerline past zero

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the 5-byte 'HTTP/' literal from the CMP buffer into the input, letting sscanf match the HTTP version pattern and increment k->headerline so subsequent iterations take the false branch of the headerline check.
one_line: I2S substitutes 'HTTP/' so headerline increments and false branch fires

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace copies multiple protocol literals (scheme 'pop3+-', host, port, 'HTTP/', header keywords) from CMP buffer into fixed input offsets, traversing all upstream gates to reach multi_done with result==CURLE_OK and rc nonzero.
one_line: I2S serially substitutes protocol literals to satisfy all upstream curl gates

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace plants 'pop3+-' at the scheme offset and 'HTTP/' at the response offset from CMP operands, driving curl through a real read/write exchange so bytecount+headerbytecount becomes nonzero and the false branch fires at line 1866.
one_line: I2S substitutes scheme and response literals to produce nonzero byte counts

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the strcmp against the 4-byte constant 'file' and substitutes it into the URL scheme offset, making strcmp return 0 and routing execution into the file: scheme handler.
one_line: I2S substitutes 'file' into URL scheme offset to pass strcmp gate

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts the 4-byte memcmp/integer-CMP matching the OpenType table tag 'GSUB' or 'GPOS' and substitutes that literal into the input table-directory offset, enabling the GSUBGPOS parser to reach the switch default arm.

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the IHDR color_type constant 0x02 (truecolor) from the CMP buffer into the input at the color_type offset, satisfying the color-type equality and reaching the num_trans nonzero conjunct inside the transformation gate.
one_line: I2S substitutes color_type=0x02 byte to satisfy IHDR truecolor gate

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the kProto* constant from the CMP buffer and substitutes it into the IPv6 next-header byte, routing HandlePayload to a non-UDP switch case that naive cannot discover by blind byte mutation.
one_line: I2S substitutes protocol code byte to route away from kProtoUdp case

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the 8-byte IPv6 header constant (including next-header 0x11 = kProtoUdp) from the CMP buffer, routing the message into the UDP/ICMP6 handler that returns a non-kErrorNone error, enabling the true branch at line 981.
one_line: I2S plants 8-byte IPv6 header literal to produce non-kErrorNone error

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the 7-byte routing-locator IID anchor (00 00 00 ff fe 00 fc) from the CMP buffer into the interface-identifier field, satisfying the IsLocator conjuncts and the m8[6] range check in one mutation.

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace pastes the 7-byte ULA prefix run and header signature from CMP operands into the input, causing UpdateIp6Route to return an error code other than kErrorNone/kErrorAddressQuery so the switch default arm is reached.
one_line: I2S plants ULA prefix bytes to drive switch past kErrorNone/kErrorAddressQuery

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace captures the mesh-local prefix and 0xFFFE IID anchor from CMP operands and substitutes them across the IPv6 address bytes, satisfying IsMeshLocalAddress and IsRoutingLocator together to take the true return at line 3835.

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace pastes Thread protocol literals (mesh-local prefix fd de ad 00 be ef at 0x19-0x1e and IID anchor ff fe 00 fc at 0x24-0x27) from the CMP buffer in one mutation step, clearing the upstream parser chain to reach GetContext with a nonzero contextId.
one_line: I2S plants dispersed Thread protocol literals to reach GetContext with nonzero id
